description_long: This bungalow features a single-story structure with a prominent gable roof covered in dark shingles. The front facade is defined by a central covered porch with a triangular pediment supported by white columns, leading up to a set of steps and a main entrance door. The exterior walls are clad in light blue horizontal siding, accented by white trim around the windows and roof edges. Large, symmetrically placed windows flank the front door, while additional windows are evenly distributed along the sides and rear, providing ample natural light. A small side porch extends from one end of the house, covered by a matching gable roof. The overall design is compact and balanced, typical of early 20th-century American residential architecture.
description: A single-story light blue bungalow with a dark gable roof, central front porch, and symmetrical white-trimmed windows. Features include a main entrance with steps, a side porch, and classic residential detailing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, central porch with triangular pediment, and two large front windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the gable end, two side windows, and the covered side porch extending from the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain facade with three evenly spaced windows and no porch or entrance.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with two windows and a clear view of the side porch roof structure.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows, evenly spaced side and rear windows
